references.name: Marketing Event Management - Event Portfolio Dashboard
references.description: Default dashboard construction pattern extracted from the Marketing Event Management Event Portfolio dashboard. Future apps must map their own business fields, metrics, filters, actions, and list sources into this structure instead of copying Marketing Event fields.

subRegions.name: Page header band
subRegions.controlPattern: container header band with title/subtitle and right-aligned filters/actions
subRegions.styleIntent: clean header band, clear page title, compact contextual actions

subRegions.name: Filter controls group
subRegions.controlPattern: horizontal filter/action row with Data Filter controls and action buttons
subRegions.styleIntent: single-line scan-friendly filters with action grouping

subRegions.name: KPI cards wrapper
subRegions.requiredWhen: KPIs or summary metrics are planned
subRegions.controlPattern: row/wrap grid of KPI cards with native FontAwesome icon tiles and live metric text
subRegions.styleIntent: consistent card surfaces, icon tile, metric label/value/trend/helper text

subRegions.name: Primary pipeline/content section
subRegions.requiredWhen: dashboard has a primary list, queue, pipeline, or portfolio region
subRegions.controlPattern: section container with header, section actions, and record display region
subRegions.styleIntent: section header with actions above the table/collection region

subRegions.name: Grid-table Collection region
subRegions.requiredWhen: tabular pipeline, queue, portfolio, register, or list display is planned
subRegions.controlPattern: Collection paired with a flex-grid header to render a grid-table display
subRegions.styleIntent: aligned header cells, collection item grid, no empty shell-only region

businessMappingContract.gridTableFieldMapping: required when tabular/list regions are planned

references.iconRule: Dashboard icons must use native FontAwesome icon controls/classes. Do not use image, SVG, emoji, or Heading/Text icon substitutes for normal dashboard UI.
references.styleAdaptationRule: Adapt colors, tokens, typography, and icons to the current Application Design System while preserving the structural pattern.
